home.aboutText1: The National Academy of the Security Service of Ukraine is the only higher military educational institution in Ukraine specializing in counterintelligence. It trains counterintelligence officers, philologists-translators, and information security experts for the Security Service of Ukraine.
home.aboutText2: The Academy was established on January 27, 1992, by a resolution of the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine. On August 2, 1999, by Decree of the President of Ukraine, the Academy was granted the status of National.
home.newsSubtitle: Current events and activities of the Academy
home.ctaText: Begin your path towards a professional career in the Security Service of Ukraine

about.title: About the Academy
about.subtitle: The only higher military educational institution in Ukraine specializing in counterintelligence
about.missionText1: The Academy's mission is to create the conditions necessary for obtaining higher and postgraduate education, as well as for conducting research in the interests of strengthening Ukraine's national security and ensuring the effective fulfillment of state security tasks.
about.missionText2: The Academy plays an active role in shaping the development and public image of the Security Service of Ukraine. It is a leader among higher education institutions in the security and defense sector and lays the foundation for attaining the status of a research university in the future.
about.missionText3: Academy values: quality, professionalism, commitment, innovation, creativity, integrity, responsibility of all participants in the educational process.

missionCards.title: Mission
missionCards.text: Creating conditions for higher and postgraduate education in the interests of strengthening national security

missionCards.title: Education
missionCards.text: Bachelor's, master's programmes, and Doctor of Philosophy training

missionCards.title: Vision
missionCards.text: A leader among higher education institutions of the security and defense sector

about.historySubtitle: Key milestones in the Academy's development

milestones.detail: On January 27, 1992, the Academy was established by a resolution of the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine to train personnel for the Security Service of independent Ukraine.
On August 2, 1999, by Decree of the President of Ukraine, the SSU Academy was granted the status of National — recognizing the highest level of training in the field of state security.
The Educational and Research Institute of Information Security was established, and students' training in cyber security and information protection was started.
Medical and tactical training for staff and cadets was implemented according to NATO standards. Strategic communications courses were launched.
The Institute was reorganized into the Educational and Research Institute of Information Security and Strategic Communications. Cybersecurity and Strategic Communications Centers were established.
Over 20 meetings held with prominent foreign experts in military, educational, and diplomatic fields. Strengthened international cooperation with EU and NATO intelligence services.

about.structureSubtitle: Educational and Research Institutes of the Academy
departments: Educational and Research Institute of Humanities — training cadets in K1 'State Security', K3 'National Security', B11 'Philology'
Educational and Research Institute of State Security — training, retraining, and professional development of security and defense sector specialists
Educational and Research Institute of Information Security and Strategic Communications — training students in cyber security, information protection, and law

admissions.title: Admissions
admissions.subtitle: Information for prospective students — how to become a cadet or student of the SSU National Academy
admissions.stepsSubtitle: Peculiarities of cadet training at the SSU Academy

steps.title: Free Education
steps.text: State-funded education with opportunity to receive a scholarship and full material support: dormitory, three meals a day, uniform, medical care

steps.title: Unique Training
steps.text: Legal and special disciplines, foreign languages, military and specialized physical training

steps.title: Military Routine
steps.text: 6-day academic week, mandatory morning physical exercise, formations, self-study, duty shifts

steps.title: 100% Employment
steps.text: After graduation, all cadets continue military service in SSU bodies and units with the rank of Lieutenant

admissions.requirementsSubtitle: Who the Academy is looking for among applicants
requirements: Motivated patriots — for whom 'patriotism' is a sincere desire to serve the state and society
Strong individuals — ready for military training, subordination, and discipline
Eager to learn — willingness and readiness to learn is the key to success and professionalism
Responsible and persistent — not afraid of challenges, able to prioritize and follow through
Physically fit — sports and physical training receive special attention throughout all years of study

specialties.name: State Security — Counterintelligence activities in the field of state security
National Security — Counterintelligence protection of state cyber security and critical infrastructure
Philology — Translation in the field of state security

admissions.datesSubtitle: Institute of Information Security and Strategic Communications (funded by individuals and legal entities)

dates.event: National Security — Cyber Protection of Information Resources; Organization of Restricted Information Protection (bachelor's/master's)
Law — Information Security Law; Information Law (bachelor's/master's)

education.title: Education
education.subtitle: Three educational and research institutes train specialists for the Security Service of Ukraine

programs.description: Training cadets in K1 'State Security', K3 'National Security', B11 'Philology'. Over 40 disciplines: specialized, legal, military-tactical, physical training, foreign languages, strategic communications.
Training at bachelor's, master's, and Doctor of Philosophy levels. Retraining of SSU personnel and advanced training of operational staff in the main functional areas of SSU activity.

programs.level: Institute of Information Security and Strategic Communications
programs.description: Training specialists in cyber security, information protection, and law. Cybersecurity and Strategic Communications Centers established. CISCO Networking Academy operational. Research laboratories for countering cyber threats.

education.featuresSubtitle: What ensures the quality of education at the Academy

features.title: Specialized Classrooms
features.text: Computer labs, forensic science and counterintelligence rooms, linguistic laboratories

features.title: Combat Training
features.text: Electronic firing simulator, off-site training facility

features.title: Language Training
features.text: Departments of Romance-Germanic, Eastern, and East European languages

features.title: CISCO Networking Academy
features.text: Practical experience in network equipment configuration, internationally recognized certificates

features.title: SSU Internships
features.text: Operational exercises and internships at the Central Directorate and regional SSU bodies

features.title: International Experience
features.text: NATO standards training, workshops with EU Advisory Mission, NATO Representation, and OSCE specialists

education.scheduleText: The SSU Academy actively participates in projects with international partners. Instructors and researchers undergo training at partner intelligence services and foreign universities. Key areas: counterintelligence, counterterrorism, applied linguistics, leadership, media and public communication.

science.title: Research
science.subtitle: The Academy's research supports the effective fulfillment of SSU tasks in safeguarding state security
science.directionsSubtitle: Key areas of scientific research at the Academy

directions.title: SSU Activities During Special Periods
directions.text: Countering intelligence-sabotage and diversionary activities of the russian federation's intelligence services and its satellites against Ukraine

directions.title: Protection of State Sovereignty
directions.text: Protection of constitutional order, territorial integrity, defense and scientific-technical potential, cyber and information security, critical infrastructure

directions.title: Counterterrorism Security
directions.text: Improving counterterrorism security in Ukraine under the legal regime of martial law

directions.title: Documentation of Russian War Crimes
directions.text: Documentation and investigation of crimes against the foundations of national security and russian war crimes

directions.title: Countering Hostile Propaganda
directions.text: Countering hostile propaganda, OSINT in the interests of state security, ensuring security in de-occupied territories

directions.title: SSU Reform to NATO Standards
directions.text: Operational and combat training system according to NATO standards, sanctions policy, strengthening SSU capabilities

publications.title: Herald of Criminal Justice
publications.description: Scientific and practical journal, Category 'B' professional publication in specialty 081 'Law'
publications.frequency: Co-founded by Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv, NAIA, SSU National Academy

science.councilsText: The SSU Academy hosts Specialized Academic Council SRD 26.706.02 for the defense of doctoral and candidate dissertations with restricted access classifications in the following specialties: 21.07.01 — ensuring state security of Ukraine (K1 State Security); 12.00.09 — criminal procedure and criminalistics, forensic examination, operative-investigative activities (D8 Law).
